notes: Colonial waterfront. Best at dusk and night — Pudong skyline across the river.
Classical Ming garden inside busy bazaar. Go weekday morning.
Tree-lined streets, art deco. Yongkang Lu for dinner.
Pedestrian shopping street. Touristy but sets the scale of Shanghai.
World-class Chinese art/antiquities. Free entry. Only if you have spare time.
Largest classical garden in China. UNESCO. Go 7:30am before crowds. ¥70.
900-year-old canal street. Walk after garden.
Leaning pagoda. Only if time allows after garden + canal.
Night cruise past lit pagodas and karst peaks. ¥150. Depart ~8pm.
Iconic karst hill shaped like elephant drinking. ¥75.
Impressive stalactite cave with coloured lighting. 5km from centre.
Dragon's Backbone terraces climbing 800m. 2h from Guilin. Needs a dedicated day.
83km cruise through karst landscape. The centrepiece of southern China. ¥210–330.
Zhang Yimou outdoor show on Li River. Spectacular. ¥238. Book ahead.
Cycle through rice fields between limestone peaks. Best way to see the karst.
Quieter than Li River. Morning mist. ¥150.
Natural moon-shaped arch in limestone hill. Hike 20 min up.
Ancient stone bridge in karst countryside. Good for sunset photos.
3000 sandstone pillars. The Avatar filming location. Park pass ¥248 (4 days).
Buddhist temple at summit, 1600 years old. The temple-on-rocks highlight of trip.
Natural arch through cliff face. 999 steps. Cable car ¥258.
Glass-floored walkway on Tianmen cliff edge. Vertigo-inducing.
World's longest glass bridge (430m). 40km from main park. Only if you want the adrenaline.
Preserved Miao/Tujia river town. Stilt houses over Tuojiang River. Entrance ¥148.
Covered wind-and-rain bridge. Centre of the old town.
Walk the restored Ming dynasty walls at dusk.
Riverside stilt district. Dramatic at night, lit up 11 stories down the cliff.
Ming/Qing dynasty old street. Good food stalls. 30 min by metro.
Line 2 train runs through an apartment building. Worth seeing.
The real Sichuan hot pot. Non-negotiable. Ask hostel for a local place.
UNESCO Buddhist rock carvings. 2h from Chongqing. Requires dedicated half-day detour.
Go 7–8am for feeding. Book ticket online (¥58). Wear neutral colours.
71m stone Buddha carved into cliff. Boat ride gives best full-body view. Train 40min from Chengdu.
Bronze Age masks from an independent civilisation. New museum 2023. Unlike anything else in China.
Tang dynasty Buddhist monastery in city centre. Peaceful. Free.
Qing dynasty lanes, now restaurants and teahouses. Touristy but pleasant evening walk.
Han dynasty-style street with snacks and lanterns. Good for an evening walk.
Impossibly turquoise lake. The single most photographed spot in Jiuzhaigou.
Deepest lake in the valley. Stunning reflections. Go early morning.
Widest high-altitude waterfall in China. Impressive volume.
Wide shallow waterfall over coloured mineral bed. Very photogenic.
String of lakes connected by boardwalks. Best intro to the valley on Day 1.
Perfectly still lake. Perfect reflections of forest and sky.
UNESCO travertine terraces at 3500m. 2h from Jiuzhaigou. Best on a longer trip.
The HK postcard. Best at dusk. Light show 8pm.
Tram up, panoramic harbour view. Night view is spectacular. Essential.
HK$3. Best 5 minutes in Hong Kong. Kowloon→Central crossing.
1847 Taoist temple. Incense coils hanging from ceiling. Hollywood Road.
Kowloon street market. Street food, fortune tellers, trinkets.
Colourful Taoist temple. Locals come for fortune telling. Metro accessible.
Giant outdoor Buddha on Lantau Island. Cable car up. Half-day trip.
Tang dynasty-style wooden Buddhist nunnery. Nan Lian Garden adjacent. Free.
Arrival hub (PVG) Day 1; also departure Day 14.
Evening departure for Guilin G-train (~7h).
Li River cruise departs 9am; 26km south of Guilin city.
Fly Chengdu Tianfu → Jiuhuang (45 min); check in 1.5h early.
Transit: fly Jiuhuang → Guangzhou → train to HK West Kowloon (1h).
